records.ety: [L. detestare, detestatum, and detestari, to curse while calling a deity to witness, to execrate, detest; de + testari to be a witness, testify, testis a witness: cf. F. dtester. See Testify.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Detested; p. pr. & vb. n. Detesting.]

defs: To witness against; to denounce; to condemn.
quotes: The heresy of Nestorius . . . was detested in the Eastern churches.
God hath detested them with his own mouth.

defs: To hate intensely; to abhor; to abominate; to loathe; as, we detest what is contemptible or evil.
quotes: Who dares think one thing, and another tell, My heart detests him as the gates of hell.
